name: Dad's Kaluski
recipeInstructions:
------------------------ONION SAUCE------------------------
1 Large Onion
4 tablespoons Butter
Salt
Pepper
KALUSKI:
1. Blend flour and salt.
2. Make a well in the center of the flour mixture.
3. Add eggs one at a time, mixing thoroughly after each addition.
4. Gradually incorporate the mashed potatoes until you have a dough that is slightly less stiff than noodle dough. If needed, add water to thin it out.
5. Roll the dough into long strands, approximately 1 inch in diameter.
6. Cut sections about 1 1/2 inches long.
7. Gently add the dough strands to a large pot of boiling water. Do not add too many at once and do not add them too quickly. Keep the water boiling. The dough will sink to the bottom when it starts to float; remove the floaters to a bowl and continue cooking until all the dough pieces are finished.
ONION SAUCE:
1. Melt butter in a pan.
2. Add coarsely sliced or diced onion.
3. Sauté until desired doneness (transparent for some, browned or almost burnt for others). Season with salt and pepper to taste.
SERVE:
There are a few serving options:
A. Pour the onion sauce over the entire batch.
B. Serve separately with plain butter for those who do not like onions.
C. As a side dish alongside meat gravy.
LEFTOVERS:
If you have leftovers, refrigerate them. They can be reheated in a frying pan for even heating; smaller pieces may heat more evenly.
